tool_definition.name: cache_poisoning_tester
tool_definition.description: Test for web cache poisoning vulnerabilities in CDN and proxy caches. Detects cache behavior, identifies unkeyed inputs, and tests for cache deception attacks. Complements the cache_poisoning.jinja prompt.

enum.value: Detect caching behavior through headers and timing analysis
Test for unkeyed header inputs that can poison cache
Test for web cache deception vulnerabilities
Test for fat GET request cache poisoning
Comprehensive cache behavior analysis with recommendations

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: Target URL to test for cache poisoning

parameter.name: header_name
parameter.description: Specific header to test (for test_headers action)

parameter.name: header_value
parameter.description: Value to inject in the test header

parameter.name: cache_buster
parameter.description: Unique value for cache busting during tests

parameter.name: deception_path
parameter.description: Path extension for cache deception testing

parameter.name: body_content
parameter.description: Body content for fat GET request testing

returns.field: Headers that affect response but not cache key
Headers whose values are reflected in response

example.name: Detect caching
example.description: Detect cache behavior through headers and timing

example.name: Test unkeyed headers
example.description: Test all common unkeyed headers for poisoning potential

example.name: Test cache deception
example.description: Test for web cache deception vulnerabilities

security_notes.note: Always use cache busters to avoid poisoning production caches
Test during low-traffic periods when possible
Document cache TTLs and test near expiration
Cache poisoning may affect many users - test responsibly
